**Happy Travelling Kangaroo Island!**

title: Kangaroo Island Wildlife Experiences
excerpt: When visiting Australia Kangaroo Island Wilderness experiences should be on your most do list. From koalas to eagles. From seals to penguins. It's all here!
content:
When it comes to tourism on Kangaroo Island you’re probably familiar with images of Remarkable rocks and Kangaroos. You might also be thinking of the empty white sandy beaches, but Kangaroo Island has so much more. 

You could be after an adventure-packed holiday with your family. Or perhaps you are looking for a relaxing romantic getaway – just the two of you, with wine tours and local food delights. And of course, there are many nature-lovers out there seeking a wildlife safari style adventure. Whatever your desire, Kangaroo Island has it all. 

**This month’s guest blog has been written by local guide Becky Prime. It is all about the unique Kangaroo Island wildlife. Becky is a nature guide at Kangaroo Island Wilderness Tours.**

Wildlife is something I am deeply passionate about and a favourite topic of mine. Every day I am still amazed by the incredible wildlife that lives in abundance – all over the 155km long and 60km wide island. There are no foxes or rabbits present over here. Therefore, we have plenty of small native mammals such as the Southern Brown Bandicoot, the Tammar wallaby and even the endangered Kangaroo Island Dunnart. Sadly, after the recent bushfires there are estimated to be only around 100 of these left. 

## Animals found on Kangaroo Island

Kangaroo Island is one of the remaining wildlife frontiers of the modern world. On any given day you may pass by a koala or 3 on the side of the road. You might even see an endangered Rosenberg Goanna searching for a meal. Then along comes a Kangaroo and her Joey munching grass, or a Wedge-Tailed Eagle soaring above you. All this may be on your drive down to the world famous Seal Bay. Here, you will be lucky enough to take a walk on the beach among the incredible endangered Australian Sea Lions – the males weighing up to 300kg! 

### Cheeky Sea Lions on Seal Bay

The cheeky antics of the juvenile Sea Lions will keep you entertained as the older females and males try to get some rest after fishing offshore. You may also spot the cutest little beach-nesting birds, the Hooded Plovers. These guys dart among the Sea Lions, trying to distract them from their well-camouflaged nest on the beach. Tiny birds such as these, are also an endangered species, yet they are commonly found on beaches all over the island – largely due to the lack of human activity on the beaches. 

### Resilient Echidnas on Kangaroo Island

Echidna’s are also a common sight on the island. They are an incredibly resilient animal and one of the two monotremes that call Kangaroo Island home. Echidna’s can live as long as 55 years and have the most incredible sense of direction. This reminds me of the story of a local gardener who removed an Echidna from his garden (it kept digging everything up!). Incredibly, he relocated it 30kms away. Just one week later, who did he find foraging around in the garden again, but the same Echidna! 

The other monotreme, being the Platypus, is also found in the rivers in the far west. But these are a little harder to spot, unless you have plenty of time to sit by the river. Of course, this is not hard to do – especially when you consider that the beauty of the natural environment on KI has largely been untouched by man. It remains as it has been for thousands of years. 

### Kangaroo Island Glossy Black-Cockatoo

Kangaroo Island is a ‘birders delight’. There are over 267 species found in varying habitats across the island. The Glossy Black Cockatoo is perhaps one of the most famous known birds on the island. In 1995 there were only around 180 birds left and their population was rapidly declining. A program was introduced that involved nesting boxes and predator prevention. Also there was a mass planting of thousands of Sheoak trees – the Glossies exclusively feed on these. It was a tremendous success. The bird population was boosted to around 400 – that is, until the devastating bushfires of last summer. 

Their current situation is unknown, as a huge amount of the Glossy habitat was burnt. A count will take place this September to see how the Glossy Black Cockatoo fared. However, there is a positive side to this story. Past strategies for boosting their numbers have been very successful. Therefore, we have a high chance of being able to do this again. 

### White bellied Sea Eagle Kangaroo Island

The majestic White Bellied Sea Eagle, although uncommon and a threatened species, is widely spread around the island’s wild coastlines. The sighting of a Sea Eagle on tour is a cause for celebration. Even though I live on the north coast, I regularly spot them flying over our local beach. It never fails to excite me if I spot one. I then watch everyone on tour enjoying the beautiful sight of this incredible bird soaring overhead. This is certainly one of the main reasons I do what I do. 

We are equally amazed by birds of a much smaller size, including the Blue Wren, Honeyeater, Spotted Pardalote, Scarlet Robin and Red-browed Finch. Each have their own delightful antics and family groups and are commonly seen in all bushland across the island. 

## Nature Touring on Kangaroo Island

Touring with KI Wilderness Tours means you get the opportunity to get off the beaten track and into private property, if need be. That way, you can often see unique wildlife behaving naturally, well away from the more popular areas. Seeing the Western Grey Kangaroo grazing in large mobs on either the north or south coast is a really special experience. 

Even if Kangaroo’s are quite common in your own neck of the woods, I find it can still be really fascinating to sit quietly and observe how they interact with each other. Kangaroo’s are very unique – not only how they use their bodies to get around, but particularly the female’s ability to put her pregnancy on hold. It helps that she has two teats in the pouch that can produce two different types of milk for their current two joeys (if they have two). 

One milk provides more fat for the younger, developing joey. The other milk is a more watered down version for the weaning Joey. It’s just amazing to think how well-suited Kangaroos are for the environment and subsequent raising of their young. On tour, we see Joeys of every size all year-round. Being a sub species of the mainland Western Grey, our Island Kangaroos are smaller, darker, shaggier and in my opinion, much cuter! 

### Meet Koalas on Kangaroo Island

If Koalas are something you dream about getting close to in the wild then Kangaroo island is the place to be. I have never been out on tour and not seen a Koala in the wild, even after this summer’s bushfires. Koalas were introduced to the island around 100 years ago in response to the declining populations on mainland Australia and they have never looked back. 

Before the summer’s events, they blossomed on the abundance of juicy Eucalyptus so much, that their numbers reached as high as 55,000. However, there is still a very healthy population on the island and most of their secret hotspots remain. **Wilderness Tours Adventure tours** still continue to roam beautifully green and abundant areas – home to the cute and cuddly Koala’s that we all dream of. 

### Wonders of the Southern Ocean

Not only do KI Wilderness Tours get off-road but we can also get offshore! Kangaroo Island is surrounded by some of the healthiest ocean ecosystems in the world. As a result, the island has quite an array of ocean wildlife. Whales migrate past the island during the winter months. Sharks abound all year round (doesn’t stop us swimming though!). There is a huge variety of smaller sea life around every underwater rock shelf. 

A favourite with locals and tourists alike, are the mystical Bottle Nose or Common Dolphins. Heading out in a boat with these agile creatures leaping out of the water in front of you, is an experience you will never forget. There is even an incredible opportunity to swim with these wild animals – sometimes dozens of them surround you – that is an opportunity not to be missed. Not even a certain local tour guide would pass that up! 

### Penguins and other wildlife on KI …

From the depths of our oceans to the midst of our wild inner bushland, there is a wildlife experience on every light step you take. Of course, there are still creatures I haven’t even had the chance to mention. One of these is the gorgeous Little Penguin, who calls our rocky shores home. Another, is the deadly but shy Tiger Snake. These guys can be seen minding their own business on sunny days throughout the year. 

The truth is, you can’t come to Kangaroo Island without having an epic wildlife experience and if you seek more you will find more. The guides at KI Wilderness Tours all have an innate gift for tracking and discovering wildlife at anytime of the year. They are all truly passionate and protective about what we have here on this unique island. 

They each have their own personal stories about the island and its wildlife. These stories add to the authenticity of your trip when you travel to Kangaroo Island. We are here with our arms open wide (but at a distance) waiting for you, when you are ready to adventure with us on this magical place we call home.

**Here are some more travel hints for planning your trip:**

- Best time to travel Australia- Best travel planning for Australia- Australia Travel Check List- Travelling between key locations- Travel Facts about Kangaroo Island- Accommodation options Kangaroo Island- Experiences with a Private Guide on KI- Kangaroo Island Regeneration after Bushfires

**Happy travelling to Kangaroo Island!**

title: Luxury Accommodation Kangaroo Island
excerpt: Kangaroo Island is a truely hidden secret for travellers to Australia. One stunning luxury accommodation Kangaroo Island offers is Oceanview Eco Villas.
content:
When it comes to the luxury accommodation Kangaroo Island has to offer, we are a little biased. Why? Because we tend to fall in love with locations and people. For us, real luxury is not defined by ‘gold-plated water taps’. Think about the time you first arrive at a special accommodation. How did it make you feel? When we visited the Ocean View Eco Villas, we felt a connection straight away – it was the right fit for us!
As professional travel designers, we have visited hundreds of accommodations. Out of these properties, there are a many that we would **not** recommend to our guests. You might be surprised at some of the reasons. First of all, some accommodations are just simply in the wrong location. Or to our way of thinking, they could be incorrectly priced. Maybe it is simply that the hosts do not have the right personality for the job. So, let’s dig a little deeper as to why we believe that Ocean View Eco Villas is a true luxury accommodation Kangaroo Island.
Beach Walk in the evening Oceanview Eco Villas

## Why stay at Oceanview Eco Villas

### 1. Kangaroo Island Travel Destination

Kangaroo Island is the third largest island off the coast of mainland Australia. In fact, you will be right in the middle of Australia’s ‘Galapagos’. The island is home to some very unique and iconic native Australian wildlife. And of course, let’s not forget Kangaroo Island’s stunning coastlines and scenery – they are nothing short of spectacular.
Location Map Kangaroo Island
The ‘locals’ believe that one of the things that makes Kangaroo Island special is the peace and serenity here. This type of tranquility can only be achieved by sharing their island home with the animals that live there.
Because Kangaroo Island is isolated from from the Australian mainland, the native species that live here have little to fear from introduced predators such as foxes.
The Primary industries on the Island are largely food producing and of course, tourism. In fact, the Island has become renowned for its quality produce, both nationally and internationally.

### 2. Staying at Oceanview Eco Villas

So lets enter the massive acreage where Oceanview Eco Villas is located. If stepping of the grid, is your kind of luxury, then you’ve probably found it here. And that ‘feeling’ I talked about earlier, only gets better as Tim and Tamsin Wendt personally welcome you. After all, there is nothing quite like that first introduction to the locals. So begins yet another luxury –  unwinding in privacy on a 500-acre coastal property.
That sense of space can be found once you step inside the Oceanview’s Eco Villas. You really do feel as if you’ve come home. The decor is so stylishly understated, but in total harmony with the natural outside environment.
Beautiful rolling hills around Oceanview Eco Villas

Each stay is fully inclusive and includes three-course dinners and a cooked breakfast. You will also have the pleasure of sampling Kangaroo Island and South Australian wines.
We would urge you to take your time in exploring the Oceanview property – to Immerse yourself in its environment. You will quickly discover that it is home to a number of native Australian wildlife and birdlife. You are bound to see Kangaroos, Wallabies and those magnificent Wedge-tailed Eagles. Now that’s one memory that will stay with you forever!

### 3. People at Oceanview Eco Villas

One thing we have found with all good hosts, is their interesting history. Since moving to Kangaroo Island 14 years ago, Tim and Tamsin have been calling Oceanview their home. Since that time, they have embraced the ‘island life’ and celebrate each day in their special slice of paradise.

Your hosts Tamsin and Tim Wendt
Having come from a background of retail management, Tim has had a long career working with people in a wide range of setting – one of which, as a policeman. More recently, he worked as a tour guide for one of the Island’s leading tour operators. He also runs a small farming enterprise on the Oceanview property.
Tamsin has trained in Clinical Psychology and she too, has worked in a number of settings. In fact, she established the first psychology practice on Kangaroo Island 12 years ago.
Tim and Tamsin have made a firm commitment to make their guests feel welcomed. What they provide is a memorable eco-luxury experience on Kangaroo Island.

### 4. Dinner at Oceanview Eco Villas

The islanders place great importance on growing their own food in a sustainable way.  You can guarantee that the food on most quality accommodations on Kangaroo Island, is locally sourced. There are some wonderful chefs on the island. Tim and Tamsin are no exception. This couple possess the necessary skills to serve up world-class cuisine. 
After a full day of touring, you’ll want to enjoy the space and privacy of your villa.  Its time to kick back and enjoy cocktails and canapes – and of course the views. What could be more perfect that enjoying a gourmet three course dinner served in the privacy of your own villa. Sounds like a perfect end to the day to me! Of course, you can also expect a two-course cooked breakfast served in your villa each morning.

The people at Oceanview Eco Villas love showing their guests what the local producers can serve up.  On a recent trip to Kangaroo Island, we sampled much of the island’s produce and believe me, it was impressive. Many of us now understand the need to keep our environmental footprint small. So Keeping it local, is everything to the islanders.
Kangaroos on the property.
By keeping it local, the islanders reducing their impact on the environment At the same time, they are cutting down transport time and distances. Like many of us, they love to support local businesses. The following, are just some of the businesses Oceanview likes to work with:

- Kangaroo Island Spirits
- Springs Road Winery
- Islander Estate Vineyards
- The Oyster Farm Shop
- KI Source
- Kangaroo Island Oats
- KI Fresh Seafood
- Emu Bay Lavender

### 5. Villa Stay at Oceanview Kangaroo Island

A stay at Oceanview Eco Villas is an exclusive and deep experience for guests – those who are seeking a more meaningful type of travel experience. To achieve this goal, Tim and Tamsin Wendt, have worked with leading eco designers, builders and interior designers. They in turn, have created a beautiful retreat, perfect for rejuvenation.
Inside one of the villas.
In fact, when you walk through the villas, it is very clear that the very best of Island craftsmen have worked their magic. From the handcrafted stone work to the cabinetry, a timeless, but contemporary design is the result. What you will get is the very best of South Australian design.
But perhaps the best Oceanview luxury can be found in the setting. Just imagine looking out across a north-facing coastline. The villas are completely off-grid, utilising solar power and the best available eco products and technology. That means ‘no noise’.
Each villa incorporates special OEV Sky Windows. So, just lie back in the privacy of your bedroom and watch the stunning sky. If long leisurely baths are your thing, open up the bifold windows and enjoy a soak while enjoying the stunning ocean views. What bliss!
**In fact, the list of special features goes on:**

- Natural timber and stone finishes
- Natural gas heating
- 100% rainwater
- Solar power
- Modwood timber decking
- Recycled jarrah timber
- Limestone paddock stonework
- Viridian smart glass windows
- Polished concrete floors providing thermal mass
- Cavalier Bremworth carpets using recycled synthetics

So you can see that as a guest, you will leave a very small footprint. How satisfying to know that you can enjoy a luxurious environment with a minimal impact on the environment. 

### 6. Tours and Experiences on Kangaroo Island

Of course, you will want to explore Kangaroo Island. Believe me this paradise has endless activities on offer. For those seeking an active adventure, you can try your hand at quad biking and adventure caving.
The island even has a sand boarding activity in the dunes. Others might be after a more ‘cruisy’ holiday – fine food and wine and a stroll along one of the many beaches. Surely, there is no better way to reflect and indulge in stunning scenery and nature.
Kangaroo Island provides endless inspiration, with its rugged, natural beauty, and abundance of native wildlife. It is home and muse to many artists, photographers and creators, and provides a source of inspiration to many who visit. View some excursion options we experienced.
**View some of the local highlights.**

- See the seals at Seal Bay Conservation Park
- Check out the Little Sahara Sand Dunes
- Visit the lighthouse at Cape Willoughby
- Say hello to the residents at Hanson Bay Wildlife Sanctuary

See here for more in-depth information on what to do on Kangaroo Island. Plus some details on the regeneration after the bushfires.
Pam and Michael on a private tour on Kangaroo Island.

### Summary on Oceanview Eco Villas

Oceanview Eco Villas represents an authentic travel experience where you meet the locals and make friends. Of course the location is stunning, but perhaps what is more important, is the harmony of all key components that sets this accommodation apart from the rest. There is none better to be found on this hidden paradise that is Kangaroo Island.
**If you are keen started with your planning or to look into these places in more detail, check out our Australia lifestyle itineraries for more information. Happy Honeymoon Travelling!**

title: Luxury Holiday Packages Australia
excerpt: Looking for an Australia Luxury Vacation Package? Exclusive Retreats? Australia Family Vacation? Exquisite 7-day, 12-day and 18-day luxury itineraries!
content:
Australia is full of hidden gems and beautiful places. When we think of Australian luxury holiday packages: privacy and personalized service always comes to mind. 

**When we think of luxury tours in Australia we think of: **

- High-end service, with caring hosts- Exquisite food and beverages- Charming boutique accommodations- Exclusive guided tours and amazing locations

These all play a major role. When you are on a luxury vacation in Australia, everything has to be perfect!

In this travel blog, we’ll talk about luxury vacation experiences, but we can’t select all of them. We have only chosen a selected few – the ones we know discerning travellers will enjoy. So let’s begin with the ‘basics’ – stunning accommodation options and exciting tours. 

We have also included a number of sample Australia luxury tour packages for you to look at, you’ll find these listed further down.

>>> View Australia itinerary packages at the end of this page now**.**

## Top 5 Exclusive Retreats Australia

Exclusive Retreats in Australia are often small in size and located in remote places. Because of these remote locations, charter flights and private transfers are often needed. 

We have listed a number of stunning places to stay. However, the Lodge of Australia will also provide up-to-date options throughout Australia. 

So here is our favourite luxury lodges in Australia…

### Seppeltsfield Vineyard Cottage Barossa Valley

We love Seppeltsfield Vineyard Cottage. This gorgeous spacious cottage is located in the Barossa Valley, South Australia’s premium wine region. The cottage is an easy one-hour drive from Adelaide on the Barossa’s historic palm-lined Seppeltsfield Road. 

What could be more idyllic than being surrounded by grapevines and lush gardens. In fact, by staying at this cottage, you will be right in the middle of the ‘Seize the Day’ premium Shiraz vineyard! 

Just a short walk away is the award-winning Hentley Farm restaurant and cellar door. 

At Seppeltsfield, you are guaranteed a truly exclusive, luxury accommodation for two. 

### Longitude 131 Ayers Rock

Longitude 131° lies at the gateway to the dual World Heritage-listed wilderness of Uluru-Kata Tjuta National Park. 

This accommodation is rather unique. Guests stay here because they are looking for an essential experience of Australia’s spiritual heartland. 

By basing yourself here, you will get a true sense of the stillness and beauty of the desert landscape. This area is rich in culture, heritage and history.

In the spirit of the original pioneers, safari-style tented pavilions have been created for your stay, but they come with a difference! Contemporary furnishings, local indigenous artwork and mod cons, will quickly dispel any notion of ‘roughing it. 

These beautiful pavilions have been designed to float over the rust-red dunes. So you can be assured of getting unparalleled views of Uluru’s changing lights. 

The sunsets and sunrises can be clearly seen from your private balcony – under the warmth of a cosy swag. 

### Saffire Freycinet Tasmania

Saffire Freycinet is Australia’s new luxury coastal sanctuary on Tasmania’s East Coast. You will be inspired by its sophistication and intimate style.

Saffire Freycinet is discreetly positioned for views over the Hazards Mountains, Freycinet Peninsula and the pristine waters of Great Oyster Bay. 

Saffire has successfully blended mankind and nature to create a breathtaking harmony.

### Oceanview Eco Retreat Kangaroo Island

You will definitely get a sense of ‘stepping off the grid’ with this one! Oceanview Eco Villas on Kangaroo Island, is a fully hosted eco-luxury experience. 

The personal touch of hosts, Tim and Tamsin Wendt is their focus.  Each villa overlooks a vast ocean – so you won’t have any trouble unwinding and rejuvenating in this understated luxury accommodation. 

In fact, you will find it hard to leave such a stunning 500-acre coastal property on beautiful Kangaroo Island.

By staying here, you will be able to explore the Oceanview property, which is home to a number of native Australian wildlife and birdlife. 

There are so many unique species to see – Kangaroos, Wallabies and Wedge-tailed Eagles, to name a few. You might even get to see the rare White-bellied Sea Eagle.

### Silky Oaks Lodge Daintree Forest

Silky Oaks Lodge is located in a stunning riverside location. It adjoins one of the oldest living rainforests, the World Heritage listed Daintree National Park. 

Of course, this makes it an ideal base from which to explore Far North Queensland.

Silky Oakes comprises an open sided Treehouse restaurant which offers fine dining. What could be more special than dining in the middle of a rainforest! 

Guests can also enjoy Silky Oaks Healing Waters Spa. Silky Oaks has just recently renovated their treehouses with contemporary and custom-designed furnishings. 

## Luxury Tours of Australia

When it comes to memorable holiday experiences, I guarantee you that hiring a private guide can make all the difference. Just imagine being collected in the morning from your hotel or lodge by a local driver-guide. 

From the word go, their focus will be totally on you and your interests. Private guides have insider information and go into more depth. They will find those special places, where you can meet local artists or winemakers. 

They will also have access to nature reserves which are only accessible by special permits. We call these guiding experiences ‘luxury’. Here is a small selection of our favourite luxury Tours of Australia…

### Best Blue Mountains Day Tour from Sydney

The Blue Mountains National Park is only a couple of hours drive from Sydney. It is a World Heritage-listed national park. Being a rugged region, the Blue Mountains are renowned for their dramatic scenery – encompassing steep cliffs and eucalyptus forests. 

You will also get waterfalls and villages dotted with guesthouses, galleries and gardens. And of course, the Blue Mountains is home to the Three Sisters sandstone rock formation.

In the morning you depart Sydney with your private guide. Your first stop is at Featherdale Wildlife Park, home to Australia’s favourite marsupials. Here, you can get up quite close to Kangaroos, Koalas, Wallabies and Wombats. 

Enjoy a fine lunch with local cuisine and local wines – all served in the heart of the Blue Mountains.

### Daintree Forest Tour from Port Douglas

The Daintree Rainforest is the oldest surviving tropical rainforest in the world. In fact, it is thought to be 165 million years old. 

Visit Cape Tribulation Beach ‘where the rainforest meets the reef’ and where two World Heritage Listed sites collide.

Meet your private guide at your accommodation. You will then be driven through the rich farming areas to the World Heritage listed Daintree National Park. 

Once you arrive at the park, you will board your own personalised craft. Now you will have a short cruise on the mirrored smooth waters of the Daintree River. As you glide quietly close to riverbanks, your guide will point out the vivid Ulysses Butterfly, Kingfishers, Pythons and other wildlife. 

Just wait until you see those enormous Crocodiles!

### Kangaroo Island Wildlife Tour (Private)

Kangaroo Island is often much larger than first-time visitors realise. The island measures about 155 kms width from west to east! So you can be assured that there are vast expanses of land to cover. 

Plus there is so much to experience here. The big attraction of Kangaroo Island is the nature experience it provides. In fact, this island is often described as the ‘Galapagos of Australia’. 

With a private nature guide, you will have a very personalized tour of this natural paradise. Last year, we enjoyed a private guided tour on Kangaroo Island. The guide’s local knowledge and insider details were second to none.

### Uluru / Ayers Rock Outback Tour

If you are staying at Longitude 131, a must-do is to join a guided Outback tour. 

Now it has to be said that Uluru / Ayers Rock is a very busy place. Therefore it is essential that you are at the right place at the right time! 

By taking a tour like this, you will also be in the company of guests who are also staying at Longitude 131. On a private tour, you will also have the luxury of touring at your own pace. 

You can either follow a set itinerary or choose a private signature experience tailor-made especially for you.

### Bruny Island & Huon Valley Tasmania

Tasmania is another popular place to visit while in Australia. Just south of the Hobart is Bruny Island. Although it is roughly the same size as Singapore, Bruny Island has a tiny population. 

It would be hard to find any more authentic food and wine experience than what is offered on Bruny Island. Spend some time on the island and discover local cheese, oyster and berry producers. 

While you are in Hobart, don’t miss out on a trip to Bruny Island and the Huon Valley – it is well worth it.

## Australia Luxury Vacation Packages

The best luxury packages are designed individually for each guest party. This is where an experienced travel designer will work his or her magic (aka Guest Australia!).

A good place to start is viewing our sample luxury travel itineraries, please see below. Naturally, any travel itinerary can be amended to your individual travel needs.

I am often asked, “where can I go?” and “what can I do?”. Well, a major part of designing your travel itinerary, will be on how much time you will have in Australia.

Remember, distances on this continent are vast. Therefore, I recommend that you have at least 2-3 nights at each location. 

Your individual interests are also key for a truly memorable ultimate luxury holiday experience in Australia. 

Our clients think we are pretty “amazing” at designing the perfect Luxury Australian Vacation, view Google Reviews.

### Australia Itinerary 7 Days – Sydney, Daintree, Ayers Rock and Melbourne

This Australia Interary (1 Week) is designed for travellers with limited time. However, rest assured that some of Australia’s top holiday spots are included. 

Think about a Sydney city tour with a harbour cruise, snorkelling the Great Barrier Reef from Port Douglas – and be stunned by the Outback around Uluru in the heart of Australia.

### 12 Days in Australia – Sydney, Daintree, Kangaroo Island Rock and Melbourne

This 12 days in Australia travel package includes:

- The magic of Kangaroo Island. If you are into nature and wildlife this is a ‘must-see’ destination. - Beside sailing Sydney harbour, you can also discover the awesome scenery of the Blue Mountain national park.- In tropical Port Douglas, get a close-up view of magnificent crocodiles.

### 18 Day Luxury Family Vacation Australia – Sydney, Daintree, Western Australia and Kangaroo Island

This 18 Days Luxury Australia family vacation is a dream itinerary. With nearly 3 weeks holiday time, you will be able to check out a lot of Australia. 

- Discover secret places in the beautiful suburbs and beaches of Sydney.- You may also want to climb the awesome Sydney Harbour Bridge! - The Daintree Forest private tour is included, as is sailing and snorkelling in style on the Great Barrier Reef. - From Perth in Western Australia, take short trips – visit the beautiful Port City of Fremantle. - Drive to the nearby Margaret River wine and food region. - Go on a Private Tour to Rottnest Island.

### Summary on Australia Luxury Vacation Packages

**Key things to consider when your are looking for a memorable luxurious Australia holiday are:** 

- Select the right locations based on your interests- Use smaller boutique accommodations and retreats- Use private guides with deep local knowledge and experience

**Here are some more travel hints for planning your trip:**

- Best time to travel Australia- Best travel planning for Australia- Australia Travel Check List- Travelling between key locations- Travel Facts about Kangaroo Island- Accommodation options Kangaroo Island- Experiences with a Private Guide on KI

**Happy travelling on your Luxury Vacation in Australia!**

title: New Zealand Australia Vacation Combined
excerpt: A New Zealand Australia vacation combined makes sense but when planning, do need to consider the varying weather and distances between these countries.
content:
Many international travellers plan a New Zealand, Australia vacation combined. This makes total sense. Surely, once you are in the South Pacific, you might as well make the most of your time there. However, there are some key differences between New Zealand and Australia. Also, lets not forget the Fiji Islands which are totally different again.

### Weather for Uluru (Ayers Road) Australia

The weather for New Zealand and Australia can vary greatly between the two countries. While both countries have winter and summer seasons, there are some Australian regions that you should really travel in the winter season! A great example of this is **Ayers Rock/ Uluru**.
Uluru (Ayers Rock) Australia
Between November and March, temperatures at Uluru can reach 50+ degrees celsius or 122+ Fahrenheit. Remember, that you won’t always be near a pool or an aircon room. Therefore, the very **best time** for the **Outback** is between **May** and **September**.

### Weather for the Daintree and Great Barrier Reef

Another example is in the northern parts of Australia. This includes the **Daintree** and **Great Barrier Reef** in the Cairns & Port Douglas areas. Again, the best time to travel this part of Australia is in winter.
Daintree Forest
The storm season here happens in ‘summer’ (Oct – March). Here is Michael in **July** last year on his visit to the Great Barrier Reef . It was idyllic and the water was so incredibly calm.
The Great Barrier Reef

### Weather for New Zealand

The best time to travel in New Zealand is between** October **and** April**. Although you can go between September and May. In fact, those months will actually fit in very well with your Australia travel seasons. However, you need to be aware that those months will be cooler. Autumn in New Zealand is the best time.
Lewis Pass
During the New Zealand ‘**winter’ season** (**June – August**) you can certainly expect low temperatures (down south to -5 degrees or 23 Fahrenheit). This will also mean that you have have shorter daylight hours. Especially in June and July, it gets dark at 5pm. So you might want to change your mind about travelling in a motor home for those months. Believe me, it can reach freezing point at nights. If you have a low-rent motor home, you won’t find it much fun to trotting off to the camp ground toilet block at 2am!

### Highs New Zealand Australia vacation combined

For many travellers, some of your **key Australian highlights** can be found in the ‘magic triangle’ – Sydney (Opera House and Bondi Beach) + Great Barrier Reef (snorkelling or diving plus Daintree Forest, time permitting) + Ayers Rock. However, with just a 5-7 day Australian visit, there will be a limit to what you can achieve.
Sydney Opera House
**In New Zealand,** most travellers are keen to see the thermal activities of the North Island. Also getting a taste of Maori culture is also a wonderful experience. Not to be missed, are the fjords around Milford Sound and the New Zealand ‘adventure capital’ Queenstown. However, visitors are tending to bypass Queenstown to stay longer at other beautiful regions. The number of visitors choosing alternative regions to Queenstown, is gaining momentum.  Not only are these other regions just as beautiful, but they are far less populated.  Of course, no one needs to miss out on the wine growing areas – just about every region has stunning vineyards.
Rippon Vineyard Wanaka

### View 21 day sample itinerary for Australia + NZ

### Distances and getting around

**Australia** is huge continent with 7.7 million square kms (or nearly 3 million square miles). Distances are vast, so realistically, you can only achieve a reasonable amount by taking domestic flights. You’re much better off with time and budget to use a 3-hour domestic flight from Sydney to Cairns – far better than a 3-day drive! Read on Getting around Australia.
**New Zealand** is much smaller (0.28 million square kms or about 0.103 square miles), but for goodness sake, do not underestimate the time it takes to drive some of the winding roads. In New Zealand, there are so many photo opportunities on the way to your next stay (the scenery is magnificent). Travellers visiting North & South Island will easily “clock-up” 4000 kms. Imagine using up your 2-week holiday with 50 hours of driving! So think about using 1-2 domestic flights or visit less regions and stay longer in each one. Read on Transport options in New Zealand.

### Time allocation for Australia and New Zealand

Out of a time budget of 3 weeks and depending on the season being travelled, I would recommend that you have at least 50/50 for each country. New Zealand has so many natural, scenic and activity highlights and travellers seriously underestimate the time needed. Check out more sample itineraries for Australia and New Zealand.

### Trans-Tasman flight connections

You will get excellent flight connections between New Zealand and Australia. Most common flights are between Auckland to Sydney or Christchurch to Brisbane. But other less known routes are Queenstown to Sydney or Auckland to Perth.

### Pricing, Accommodation and visa information

Prices between New Zealand and Australia are fairly similar. In respect of accommodation options, Australia has the edge with some of the most amazing lodges, retreats and island resorts.

### Visas for New Zealand Australia vacation combined

With the exception of New Zealand passport holders, all travellers will require an **Australia visa – **even if you’re in transit for longer than 4 hours. So it probably pays to check with your airline before you make any reservations into or via Australia. View this Australia check-list.
As far as **New Zealand **goes, most European and North American and some Asian countries have visa-free policies for stays up to 90 days. Again, you will need to double-check this prior to your departure. Remember, your passport needs to be valid for at least 6 month after your departure date!
**Happy Travelling!**

title: Perth Attractions
excerpt: Whether you're a first-time visitor or returning to explore more, this guide highlights my personal
content:
Whether you’re a first-time visitor or returning to explore more, this guide highlights my personal favorite **Perth attractions** that offer something for everyone. Perth, the sunniest capital city in Australia, is a vibrant destination bursting with culture, history, natural beauty, and unique experiences. From free city walking tours and hidden foodie gems to stunning beaches, wildlife encounters, and even a rock and roll legend’s hometown, Perth is a city full of surprises just waiting to be uncovered.

My name is Michael, and as someone who has traveled extensively through Western Australia, I’m excited to share these top ten must-see places and activities in Perth. Let’s dive into a journey that blends local insights, cultural discoveries, and scenic escapes, perfect for travelers seeking authentic experiences beyond the usual tourist trail.

## 1. Explore Perth with a Free Guided City Walking Tour

One of the best ways to get acquainted with Perth is by joining the **free guided city walking tours** offered by the City of Perth. These tours run Monday to Saturday, twice daily at 9:45 AM and 2 PM, departing from the I City kiosk in Forest Place. Each walk lasts about 90 minutes, and the best part is, no booking is required—you just show up ready to explore.

You can select from themed tours such as an orientation walk, an art-focused tour, or a historic exploration. This variety allows visitors to tailor their experience based on interests, whether it’s discovering Perth’s urban development, admiring public artworks, or stepping back in time to understand the city’s rich heritage.

Walking through the city streets with a local guide offers a deeper connection to Perth, as you hear stories and facts you might miss on your own. Plus, it’s a fantastic way to get your bearings and plan the rest of your trip.

## 2. Discover Northbridge: Perth’s Cultural and Culinary Hotspot

If you want to uncover Perth’s coolest cultural pocket, Northbridge is the place to be. This vibrant suburb is a treasure trove of street art, heritage sites, and local favorites. Joining a Northbridge walking tour with a knowledgeable guide lets you explore laneways adorned with colorful murals, stop at buzzing cafés, and delve into the multicultural history that shapes this dynamic neighborhood.

One highlight is the ReStore, a Northbridge icon known for its old-school Italian deli, gourmet foods, and excellent coffee. It’s a must-visit for food lovers and anyone curious to experience the authentic flavors and community vibe of Perth.

Whether you choose to explore independently or with a guide, Northbridge offers an immersive experience that goes beyond conventional sightseeing. It’s perfect for cultural seekers who want to taste, see, and feel the heart of Perth’s creative spirit.

## 3. Immerse Yourself in Culture at the Western Australian Museum and Art Gallery

Located near Northbridge, the Western Australian Museum and Art Gallery are two exceptional venues that bring Perth’s culture, history, and creativity to life. These institutions are ideal for art lovers, history buffs, and anyone eager to understand Western Australia on a deeper level.

The museum showcases the natural and cultural heritage of the region, with exhibits covering everything from ancient Aboriginal culture to marine life and geology. The Art Gallery features contemporary and traditional works from Western Australian artists and international creators, providing insight into the artistic landscape that shapes the city’s identity.

Visiting these sites is a wonderful way to spend a few hours indoors, especially if you want to enrich your knowledge about Perth’s past and present. Both venues are accessible, engaging, and offer rotating exhibitions that keep the experience fresh for repeat visitors.

## 4. Experience Breathtaking Views and Nature at Kings Park and Botanic Garden

No trip to Perth is complete without visiting Kings Park and Botanic Garden, one of the world’s largest inner-city parks. The park offers stunning views over the city skyline and the Swan River, combined with native bushland, floral displays, memorials, and scenic outlooks.

For a richer experience, join the **free guided walking tour** led by knowledgeable local volunteers. Tours run daily at 10 AM, departing from Fraser Avenue near the visitor information center, and last around 90 minutes. These walks provide fascinating insights into Western Australia’s biodiversity, history, and the cultural significance of the land.

Whether you’re a nature enthusiast or simply looking for a peaceful escape in the heart of the city, Kings Park is a must-visit. Its blend of natural beauty and urban backdrop makes it a photographer’s paradise and a favorite spot for locals and tourists alike.

## 5. Explore Central Perth Gems: Elizabeth Quay and Government House

Right in the heart of Perth lies Elizabeth Quay, a waterfront precinct that beautifully blends modern architecture, public art, and a lively atmosphere with bars, cafés, and restaurants. It’s a great place to relax, enjoy the river views, and soak up the city vibe.

Just a short walk from Elizabeth Quay is Government House, a hidden gem featuring elegant Victorian architecture and peaceful gardens. The house offers free public access on selected days, providing visitors a glimpse into Western Australia’s colonial history and heritage.

This combination of urban charm and historic tranquility makes for a delightful day of exploration without leaving the city center. It’s perfect for travelers who appreciate both cultural heritage and contemporary city life.

## 6. Hold History in Your Hands at the Perth Mint

Ever wondered what it feels like to hold a real gold bar? At the Perth Mint, you can do just that. This iconic institution not only showcases dazzling gold bars but also offers live demonstrations where you can watch molten gold being poured right before your eyes.

The Perth Mint is much more than a museum; it’s a treasure trove of history, sparkle, and surprises. It tells the wild stories of Western Australia’s gold rush days, making it a fascinating visit for history enthusiasts and families alike.

Tours run six times a day, every hour from 9:30 AM onwards. Due to its popularity, I recommend booking your tickets ahead of time to secure your spot.

## 7. Dive into Fremantle’s History, Art, and Rock ‘n’ Roll Vibes

Just a 30-minute trip from Perth’s city center is Fremantle, a coastal gem known for its rich history, vibrant street life, and artistic energy. Fremantle is also the hometown of the legendary rock and roll frontman Bon Scott of AC/DC, whose passionate singing style is commemorated with a statue in the harbor area—a must-visit for music fans.

To truly experience Fremantle’s charm, join a free walking tour led by passionate local volunteers who bring the city’s stories to life. These tours run daily at 11 AM from the Fremantle Information Center at King’s Square, and no booking is necessary.

Wander through Fremantle’s heritage buildings, lively markets, and art galleries while soaking up the coastal atmosphere. This town perfectly blends history, culture, and seaside relaxation.

## 8. Relax and Surf at Scarborough and Cottesloe Beaches

When it comes to beaches near Perth, Scarborough and Cottesloe stand out as two of Western Australia’s most iconic coastal spots. Both are easily accessible by bus or train from Perth’s CBD, making them perfect for a day trip.

**Scarborough Beach** is a favorite for surfers and those who love oceanfront walks. Don’t miss the Scarborough beach pool, located right by the waves—it’s a beautiful spot to swim and enjoy the ocean breeze.

**Cottesloe Beach**, on the other hand, offers calm turquoise waters, Norfolk pine-lined lawns, and some of the most famous Indian Ocean sunsets. It’s ideal for families, swimmers, and anyone looking for a picturesque beachside experience.

Both beaches provide a quintessential Perth coastal experience and are great places to unwind after exploring the city.

## 9. Escape to Rottnest Island: A Car-Free Paradise

Just a 30-minute ferry ride from Fremantle lies Rottnest Island, a laid-back and car-free paradise. This island is full of adventure and natural beauty, featuring over 60 pristine beaches to explore.

Rent a bike to discover the island’s hidden coves, join guided tours to learn about its history and wildlife, or snorkel in the crystal-clear waters. One of the island’s happiest residents is the quokka, a small marsupial famous for its friendly demeanor and photogenic smile.

Daily ferries depart from Perth’s Barracks in the CBD, Fremantle, or Hillarys, with return options in the afternoon or evening. This makes for a perfect full-day excursion, whether you’re into wildlife, history, or simply soaking up the sun.

## 10. Sip and Savor in Swan Valley, Western Australia’s Oldest Wine Region

Only 25 minutes from Perth’s CBD, Swan Valley is the state’s oldest wine region and an ideal spot for a relaxing day trip. The region is renowned for its boutique wineries, craft breweries, chocolate makers, coffee roasters, and artisan producers.

You can join a guided wine tour or explore the area on a self-drive adventure. Many tours offer hotel pickup and lunch, making it easy and stress-free to enjoy the day. Whether you’re a wine connoisseur or simply looking to indulge in local flavors, Swan Valley has something to delight every palate.

## Bonus: Experience the Outback at Pinnacles Desert

If you want to experience a bit of the Australian outback while still based in Perth, the Pinnacles Desert in Nambung National Park is a jaw-dropping destination. About two and a half hours’ drive from Perth, this landscape features ancient limestone spires rising dramatically from yellow sands.

Daily tours with city pickups are available, letting you explore the desert on foot or by scenic drive. Along the way, you might spot wild kangaroos and emus, adding to the authentic outback experience. For stargazers, there’s also an evening tour that offers spectacular views of the night sky.

The Pinnacles Desert is a unique attraction that contrasts beautifully with Perth’s urban and coastal environments, making it a memorable addition to your Western Australia itinerary.

## Conclusion: Your Ultimate Guide to Perth Attractions

Perth is a multifaceted city that offers much more than just sunshine. From immersive walking tours and cultural neighborhoods to stunning natural parks, pristine beaches, and historic sites, the city and its surrounds provide countless opportunities for adventure and discovery.

Whether you’re exploring the vibrant streets of Northbridge, holding a gold bar at the Perth Mint, catching a wave at Scarborough Beach, or cycling among quokkas on Rottnest Island, each experience adds a unique chapter to your Perth story.

Plan your visit with these top ten **Perth attractions** in mind, and you’re sure to have a trip filled with unforgettable moments, rich culture, and breathtaking landscapes. Perth truly is a destination that invites you to explore, learn, and relax all at once.

Happy travels!

title: Plan Your Australia in 2 Weeks: A Comprehensive Travel Itinerary
excerpt: Discover the best of Australia in just 2 weeks with our comprehensive travel itinerary. Explore iconic cities, stunning landscapes, and unique wildlife.
content:
Embarking on an adventure to Australia in 2 weeks is an exciting opportunity to explore stunning landscapes, vibrant cities, and unique wildlife. This guide will provide you with a detailed itinerary to make the most of your trip, covering must-visit locations and essential travel tips.

## Overview of the 2-Week Itinerary

Ready to experience the best of Australia in 2 weeks? This comprehensive itinerary covers key highlights, from bustling cities to natural wonders. Here’s a quick rundown of your adventure:

- Sydney: 3 nights

- Port Douglas: 3 nights

- Kangaroo Island: 3 nights

- Great Ocean Road: 3 nights

- Melbourne: 1 night

This carefully crafted plan balances urban exploration with breathtaking natural landscapes, giving you a taste of Australia’s diverse offerings.

## Choosing the Best Time to Visit

Timing is crucial when planning your Australia in 2 weeks trip. The country’s vast size means climate varies significantly between regions.

### Northern and Central Australia

For the northern parts, including the Great Barrier Reef, aim for April to October. This avoids the rainy season (December to March) and scorching temperatures in places like Uluru.

### Southern Australia

Southern regions like Adelaide, Kangaroo Island, and Melbourne are best visited from October to April, during the Australian summer.

### Ideal Months

For this specific itinerary, which covers both north and south, the sweet spots are April/May or September/October. These shoulder seasons offer the best overall conditions across all destinations.

## Key Locations to Include in Your Trip

This itinerary focuses on must-see destinations that showcase Australia’s diversity:

### Sydney

Iconic landmarks, beautiful beaches, and vibrant culture make Sydney an essential stop. Don’t miss the Opera House, Harbour Bridge, and Bondi Beach.

### Port Douglas

Gateway to two World Heritage sites: the Great Barrier Reef and Daintree Rainforest. Perfect for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

### Kangaroo Island

Often called Australia’s Galapagos, it’s a wildlife haven with stunning landscapes. Expect to see kangaroos, koalas, and sea lions in their natural habitat.

### Great Ocean Road

One of the world’s most scenic coastal drives, featuring the famous Twelve Apostles and charming towns like Port Fairy and Apollo Bay.

### Melbourne

Australia’s cultural capital, known for its laneways, coffee culture, and vibrant arts scene. It’s the perfect place to wrap up your journey.

## Detailed Day-by-Day Itinerary Overview

Let’s break down this exciting Australia in 2 weeks adventure day by day:

### Days 1-3: Sydney

Explore the heart of Australia’s largest city, taking in its iconic sights and laid-back beach culture.

### Days 4-6: Port Douglas

Dive into the natural wonders of tropical North Queensland, including the Great Barrier Reef and Daintree Rainforest.

### Days 7-9: Kangaroo Island

Immerse yourself in Australia’s unique wildlife and rugged coastal scenery.

### Days 10-12: Great Ocean Road

Embark on an unforgettable road trip along one of the world’s most scenic coastal routes.

### Day 13: Melbourne

Wrap up your journey in Australia’s cultural capital, soaking in its unique atmosphere.

## Day 1: Arrival in Sydney

Welcome to Sydney! Your Australia in 2 weeks adventure begins in this vibrant harbor city.

### Accommodation

Base yourself in the city center for easy access to key attractions. Consider these options:

- Grace Hotel: Mid-range, centrally located

- The Langham: Overlooking Darling Harbour

- Capella Sydney Hotel: For a truly luxurious experience

### Getting Over Jet Lag

Combat jet lag with a leisurely walk around the city. It’s a great way to start acclimatizing and get your bearings.

## Day 2: Exploring Sydney

Dive into Sydney’s iconic sights and vibrant neighborhoods.

### Morning: City Tour

Start with a guided city tour to cover major attractions efficiently. Don’t miss:

- Sydney Opera House

- Sydney Harbour Bridge

- The Rocks historic area

- Bondi Beach

### Afternoon: Harbour Cruise

Take a ferry to Manly Beach, enjoying stunning harbour views along the way. Spend some time exploring Manly’s laid-back beach atmosphere.

### Evening: Dinner with a View

Cap off your day with dinner at a restaurant overlooking Darling Harbour or Circular Quay for breathtaking night views of the city.

## Day 3: Day Trip to the Blue Mountains

Escape the city for a day of nature and adventure in the nearby Blue Mountains.

### Morning: Scenic World

Start at Scenic World for panoramic views of the Three Sisters and Jamison Valley. Experience the world’s steepest incline railway and the Scenic Skyway.

### Afternoon: Bushwalking

Embark on a guided bushwalk to immerse yourself in the unique Australian flora and fauna. Keep an eye out for native birds and maybe even a wallaby!

### Evening: Sunset at Echo Point

Return to Echo Point lookout for a spectacular sunset over the Three Sisters before heading back to Sydney.

## Day 4: Flight to Cairns and Transfer to Port Douglas

Say goodbye to Sydney and hello to tropical North Queensland!

### Morning: Flight to Cairns

Catch an early flight from Sydney to Cairns (about 3 hours). Upon arrival, transfer to Port Douglas (about 1 hour drive).

### Afternoon: Wildlife Habitat Centre

If time allows, visit the Wildlife Habitat Centre just outside Port Douglas. It’s a fantastic introduction to Australia’s unique wildlife, perfect for all ages.

### Evening: Explore Port Douglas

Settle into your accommodation and take a stroll around Port Douglas. Enjoy dinner at one of the restaurants along Macrossan Street, the town’s main thoroughfare.

### Accommodation Options in Port Douglas

- Peninsula Boutique Hotel (adults only)

- Shantara Resort (adults only)

- Mandalay Apartments (great for families)

With this itinerary, you’re set for an unforgettable Australia in 2 weeks journey, covering the country’s highlights from city to reef to outback. Each day brings new adventures and opportunities to immerse yourself in Australia’s diverse landscapes and cultures.

## Days 5-6: Discovering the Great Barrier Reef and Daintree Rainforest

Your australia in 2 weeks adventure continues with two full days exploring the natural wonders of tropical North Queensland.

### Day 5: Great Barrier Reef Excursion

Today is dedicated to one of Australia’s most iconic attractions – the Great Barrier Reef.

- Join a tour with Sailaway Port Douglas for a day of snorkeling or diving

- Marvel at the vibrant coral formations and diverse marine life

- Option for certified divers: Book a diving excursion with Calypso

Remember to apply reef-safe sunscreen and stay hydrated throughout the day.

### Day 6: Daintree Rainforest Adventure

Venture north of Port Douglas to explore the ancient Daintree Rainforest, one of the oldest forests in the world.

- Take a guided nature walk through lush tropical vegetation

- Spot unique wildlife, including colorful birds and possibly crocodiles

- Learn about the rainforest’s ecological importance and Aboriginal cultural significance

The contrast between reef and rainforest showcases Australia’s incredible biodiversity.

## Day 7: Flight to Adelaide and Transfer to Kangaroo Island

Today marks a transition from tropical north to the rugged south as you journey to Kangaroo Island.

### Morning: Departure from Port Douglas

- Take an early transfer back to Cairns Airport

- Fly to Adelaide (flight time varies, typically 3-4 hours)

### Afternoon: Onward to Kangaroo Island

Connecting to Kangaroo Island (locally known as ‘KI’) requires some planning:

- Currently, only Qantas offers scheduled flights to KI

- Flight time from Adelaide to KI is approximately 40 minutes

- If flight schedules don’t align, you may need to overnight in Adelaide

Pro tip: Book flights well in advance, as schedules can be limited.

### Alternative: Ferry Option

If flights are unavailable, consider the ferry service:

- Departs from Cape Jervis (about 2 hours drive from Adelaide)

- Ferry crossing takes about 45 minutes

- Longer journey, but offers scenic coastal views

Whichever option you choose, you’re in for a dramatic change of scenery!

## Days 8-9: Exploring Kangaroo Island

Welcome to Kangaroo Island, often called Australia’s Galapagos for its unique wildlife and stunning landscapes.

### Accommodation

Treat yourself to some of Australia’s most spectacular eco-retreats:

- Ecopia Retreat Eco Villas: Luxurious sustainability in the heart of nature

- Oceanview Echo Retreat: Breathtaking coastal views and modern comforts

### Day 8: Wildlife and Nature

- Visit Seal Bay Conservation Park to see Australian sea lions in their natural habitat

- Explore Flinders Chase National Park, home to iconic rock formations like Remarkable Rocks

- Spot kangaroos, wallabies, and possibly even echidnas in the wild

### Day 9: Coastal Wonders

- Take a guided tour to Admirals Arch to see fur seals and stunning coastal scenery

- Visit local producers like Clifford’s Honey Farm or Kangaroo Island Spirits

- End the day with a sunset walk along one of the island’s pristine beaches

Kangaroo Island’s untouched beauty and abundant wildlife make it a highlight of any australia in 2 weeks itinerary.

## Days 10-12: The Great Ocean Road Adventure

Embark on one of the world’s most scenic coastal drives as you journey along the Great Ocean Road.

### Day 10: Adelaide to Mount Gambier

- Fly back to Adelaide and pick up your rental car

- Drive to Mount Gambier (about 4.5 hours)

- Visit the stunning Blue Lake, known for its vibrant color changes

### Day 11: Mount Gambier to Port Fairy

- Start early and drive to Port Fairy (about 2.5 hours)

- Explore the charming historic town and its beautiful beaches

- Optional detour: Visit the Grampians National Park for hiking and Aboriginal rock art

### Day 12: Port Fairy to Apollo Bay

- Drive the most scenic part of the Great Ocean Road (about 3 hours)

- Stop at the iconic Twelve Apostles rock formations

- Visit Loch Ard Gorge and learn about its shipwreck history

- End your day in the seaside town of Apollo Bay

The Great Ocean Road offers a perfect blend of natural wonders and charming coastal towns, making it a must-do on your australia in 2 weeks journey.

## Day 13: Final Day in Melbourne

Conclude your road trip and immerse yourself in Australia’s cultural capital.

### Morning: Drive to Melbourne

- Early start for the drive from Apollo Bay to Melbourne (about 3 hours)

- Return your rental car upon arrival

### Afternoon: Melbourne Exploration

- Take a stroll through the famous laneways, known for street art and cafes

- Visit Federation Square, the city’s cultural hub

- Optional: Take a tour of the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG)

### Evening: Farewell Dinner

Cap off your australia in 2 weeks adventure with a memorable meal in one of Melbourne’s world-class restaurants.

## Day 14: Departure or Extended Stay

Today marks the end of your whirlwind australia in 2 weeks journey. Depending on your plans, you have several options:

- Depart from Melbourne International Airport for your return flight

- Take a domestic flight back to Sydney if your international flight departs from there

- Extend your stay to explore more of Melbourne or nearby regions

## Additional Travel Options and Considerations

If you have more time or are looking to customize your australia in 2 weeks itinerary, consider these options:

### New Zealand Extension

Many travelers combine Australia and New Zealand into one trip. If this interests you, check out dedicated itineraries that cover both countries efficiently.

### Tasmania for Hiking Enthusiasts

If you love hiking and pristine wilderness, Tasmania offers some of Australia’s best trails and national parks. It’s perfect for outdoor enthusiasts looking to extend their trip.

### Seasonal Considerations

Remember that Australia’s seasons are opposite to those in the Northern Hemisphere. Always check weather conditions for your specific travel dates and destinations.

### Flexibility is Key

Australia is vast and diverse. While this australia in 2 weeks itinerary covers key highlights, don’t be afraid to adjust based on your interests. Whether it’s spending more time in the tropical north or exploring the wine regions near Adelaide, tailor the trip to your preferences.

This australia in 2 weeks itinerary offers a taste of the country’s incredible diversity, from iconic cities to natural wonders. It’s designed to maximize your time while providing unforgettable experiences across this vast and beautiful land.

### Additional Travel Resources

To further enhance your trip, here are some valuable resources:

- Download the **100% FREE NZ Travel Planning Sheet** here

- Watch our **NZ Travel Planning Playlist** on YouTube here

- Review our **NZ Sample Travel Itineraries** on YouTube here

- Check out our **NZ Road Trips Playlist** for route inspiration here

- Visit key **NZ Travel locations** on our playlist here

- Explore our **NZ Must Do & See Playlist** here

**Self-drive Road Trips:**

Christchurch to Lake Tekapo

Lake Tekapo to Mt Cook National Park

Lake Tekapo to Dunedin

Dunedin to Invercargill

Invercargill to Te Anau

Queenstown to Milford Sound

Punakaiki to Franz Josef

Nelson to Punakaiki

title: Things to do in Australia: A Practical Two-Week Guide
excerpt: Only have 14 days in Australia? This practical guide shows where to go, when to visit (reef & outback seasons), realistic day counts, and top sights from Sydney to the Great Barrier Reef and Uluru.
content:
Australia is huge, diverse, and full of iconic experiences. If you’re planning a first trip, the biggest mistake is cramming too much into too little time or visiting the wrong places at the wrong time of year. This guide focuses on the best **things to do in Australia**, when to visit each region, and how to assemble a realistic two-week itinerary that leaves you satisfied rather than exhausted.

## Sydney: Your Easy Win and Perfect Starting Point

Sydney is where most international flights land, which makes it the natural place to start. It’s one of the top **things to do in Australia** for good reason: a compact city centre, world-class landmarks, and beaches within easy reach.

Plan to stay **two to three nights**. That gives you time for the essentials without feeling rushed:

- Sydney Opera House (take a guided tour or catch a performance)

- Walk across or around the Sydney Harbour Bridge

- Relax at Bondi Beach or try the Bondi to Coogee coastal walk

- Explore neighborhoods: The Rocks, Darling Harbour, Surry Hills

Most visitors also underestimate how close the Blue Mountains are. The Blue Mountains are a UNESCO World Heritage area—about a 90-minute drive from the city—featuring dramatic sandstone cliffs, eucalyptus forests, and the Three Sisters rock formations. If you like scenic railways, short hikes, and lookout points, add a day trip to your Sydney stay. It’s one of those **things to do in Australia** that feels wildly different from the urban energy of the city.

## Great Barrier Reef and Tropical North: When Timing Matters

The Great Barrier Reef stretches more than 2,300 km along the east coast, but for snorkelers and divers, the most reliable reef experience is in the far north near Cairns and Port Douglas. This is where many travellers tick off a top **things to do in Australia** item: swimming among vibrant coral and tropical fish.

Timing is critical. Visit between **April and October**—the dry season. Outside those months you risk rain, humidity, and dangerous jellyfish in the water. For safety and comfort, plan your reef time during the dry season.

My practical tips for the reef:

- Base yourself in Port Douglas if you can. It’s quieter, feels more local, and often has shorter boat runs to quality outer-reef sites than Cairns.

- Allow **three to four nights** for the region. That gives you time for a full-day reef trip, a Daintree rainforest visit, and a relaxed day to recover.

- Book a certified operator for snorkeling or diving—reef health and safety standards vary.

Beyond the reef, the Daintree Rainforest—believed to be the oldest rainforest on Earth—offers guided walks, river cruises, and the Kuranda Scenic Railway. If you want a taste of the outback without a huge detour, there are inland trips from Port Douglas or Cairns to limestone caves and classic outback landscapes.

## Uluru and the Red Centre: Iconic but Consider the Trade-Offs

Uluru (Ayers Rock) is one of the most recognisable images of Australia. It’s iconic, but there are practical realities that every traveller should know before committing a chunk of a two-week trip to it.

Key considerations:

- Uluru is extremely remote. Travel and accommodation costs are higher than coastal alternatives.

- Best visiting months are **April to October**. Outside that window, summer temperatures can soar to 40–45°C (over 110°F).

- Uluru can feel very touristy during peak season, with large tour groups at the main viewpoints.

If the true outback vibe is the goal and you have the time and budget, consider alternatives:

- Darwin region: Kakadu and Litchfield National Parks offer wetlands, waterfalls, crocodiles, and ancient Aboriginal rock art.

- Kimberley region: Remote, rugged, and spectacular—best for travellers who can commit extra time and a higher budget.

For many first-time visitors limited to two weeks, the outback portion becomes the most difficult to fit in without compromising other highlights. If you must choose, weigh the cost and travel time against how much you value seeing Uluru specifically.

## Wildlife: Kangaroos, Koalas and the Best Place to See Them

Meeting a kangaroo or holding a koala is high on many wish lists. The truth is kangaroos are common across much of Australia—sometimes even considered pests by locals—yet encountering wildlife in natural settings still ranks among the top **things to do in Australia**.

My single best recommendation for wildlife is **Kangaroo Island**. Off the southern coast near Adelaide, it is often called the Galapagos of Australia for good reason:

- See kangaroos, wallabies, koalas in the wild, sea lions, and fur seals

- Rare and abundant bird life plus dramatic coastal scenery

- Accessible: about a 30-minute flight from Adelaide or a ferry transfer

Best time to visit Kangaroo Island is **October to April**. Plan at least **three nights** to explore properly. If you’re deciding between Uluru and a nature-rich island escape on a constrained budget and schedule, Kangaroo Island is often the smarter choice—easier access, lower cost, and authentic wildlife encounters without the crowds.

## Road Tripping: The One Drive I Recommend

Australia’s distances are vast, so not all road trips are practical for a two-week route. For first-time visitors with limited time, the standout drive is the **Great Ocean Road** in Victoria.

Why it’s special:

- Coastal cliffs, rainforest pockets, and the Twelve Apostles limestone stacks

- Charming seaside towns, lighthouse views, and plenty of short walks

To do the Great Ocean Road justice, aim for at least **three nights**, ideally five. Day trips from Melbourne are possible but long; overnight stops make the scenery and small towns enjoyable rather than rushed.

## Tasmania: A Different Side of Australia

Tasmania is often overlooked, yet it offers mountain ranges, pristine beaches, and hiking that rivals New Zealand. If your interests lean toward hiking, food, and dramatic landscapes, Tasmania should be high on the list of **things to do in Australia**.

Top Tasmanian highlights:

- Cradle Mountain for alpine hikes

- Wineglass Bay for coastal vistas

- Bay of Fires for orange lichen-covered rocks and clear waters

Best time to visit is **October to April**. You’ll need at least **four to five nights** to experience Tasmania’s main natural attractions.

## Putting Together a Realistic Two-Week Itinerary

Travel in shoulder seasons—April, May or September, October—if possible. These months often offer great weather across multiple regions, fewer crowds, and reasonable prices. When planning, follow three simple rules:

- Stay at least **two to three nights** in each destination.

- Use domestic flights to connect distant regions efficiently.

- Use transfers or shuttles where practical instead of renting a car everywhere.

Sample two-week itinerary (balanced, realistic, and season-friendly):

- Days 1–3: Sydney — city highlights and a Blue Mountains day trip

- Days 4–7: Fly to Port Douglas — Great Barrier Reef day trips, Daintree rainforest, optional outback cave experience

- Days 8–10: Fly to Adelaide and transfer to Kangaroo Island — wildlife, coastal drives, relaxed pace

- Days 11–13: Return to Adelaide and drive or transfer for the Great Ocean Road — experience the coastal scenery and 12 Apostles

- Day 14: Transfer to Melbourne for departure

This plan covers the major icons: city, reef, wildlife, and a classic coastal drive, while keeping travel time and costs manageable. You can swap Kangaroo Island for Tasmania if you prefer mountains and hikes, or add Uluru only if you can extend the trip and budget.

### Practical Planning Tips

- **Book domestic flights early**. Australia is big, and flights connect long-distance hops. Early bookings save time and money.

- **Pack for variety**. Coastal tropics are hot and humid; Tasmania and alpine areas are cool. Layering is essential.

- **Respect local seasons**. Reef and tropical regions are best April–October; southern coasts and Tasmania shine October–April.

- **Balance activity with downtime**. Two to three nights per place reduces travel fatigue and gives time to explore at a comfortable pace.

- **Consider transfers** and short regional shuttle services instead of renting a car everywhere—saves cost and hassle in cities and reef gateways.

## Why This Approach Works

When deciding which **things to do in Australia** to prioritise, focus on contrast and logistics. Mix a major city (Sydney) with a natural icon (the Great Barrier Reef), wildlife immersion (Kangaroo Island), and a scenic drive (Great Ocean Road). That combination gives a broad sense of Australia’s variety in two weeks without constant long-haul travel between each stop.

### Quick Seasonal Cheat Sheet

- **April–October** — Best for Great Barrier Reef, Uluru, and northern dry season activities

- **October–April** — Best for Kangaroo Island, Tasmania, and southern coastal escapes

- **Year round** — Sydney is visitable any time, but summers (December–February) are hotter and busier

## Final Thoughts

Australia offers a staggering range of experiences, and wisely choosing which **things to do in Australia** will make your trip feel intentional rather than frantic. Aim for quality time in each place, prioritise seasons, and connect distant regions with flights. Whether you want reef snorkelling, kangaroos in the wild, dramatic coastal drives, or mountain hikes, a thoughtfully planned two-week trip can deliver authentic and memorable experiences.

If you prefer wildlife and coastal scenery without the high cost and remoteness of the Red Centre, consider prioritising Kangaroo Island and Tasmania over Uluru. If reef time is non-negotiable, slot that early in the dry season to get the best conditions.

Happy planning—and enjoy the best **things to do in Australia** on a trip that actually leaves you wanting to come back for more.

title: Top Things To Do In Port Douglas And Surrounds
excerpt: Find out more about travel information on Port Douglas. This place in northern Queensland has lots to offer for a stay up to a week!
content:
## Top Things To Do In Port Douglas And Surrounds

Port Douglas is the gateway to a large number of key tourism destinations in Australia. The most well-known Port Douglas attractions include the famous Great Barrier Reef and the ancient Daintree Forest, and many more – forming an extensive list of must-do’s for any Australian holiday itinerary. 

As with any holiday there some key considerations when planning your trip to Port Douglas. Read on for information on the best things to do in Port Douglas, detailed travel information and accommodation options. 

## Top 7 Things To Do In Port Douglas

A fabulous holiday destination and beautiful place to visit, Port Douglas has lots to see and do, and plenty of fun for all ages. The area is an outdoor enthusiast’s dream holiday location with national treasures such as The Great Barrier Reef and the lush Daintree Rain Forest right at your doorstep. 

Wondering what to do while in Port Douglas? Take a look at our top 6 things to do! Want to make the most of the outdoor activities in Port Douglas? Taking a guided tour is a great way to enjoy these activities – you’ll see and learn so much more. And there’s nothing better than kicking back and letting someone else do the driving! Read on for the top things to do in Port Douglas. 

### 1. Hug A Koala At Wildlife Habitat Port Douglas

This epic Australian Wildlife experience is perfect for those who want to hold a koala and hand feed a kangaroo. These are just two of the animals you will get to meet on your visit to the Wildlife Habitat at Port Douglas, others include pythons, crocodiles, wallabies and a selection of Australian birds all housed in natural settings. The facility also operates a wildlife conservation and rescue center. 

### 2. Swing Through The Jungle With Jungle Surfing Canopy Tours

Enjoy a guided zip lining tour through the stunning Daintree Rainforest with the team at Jungle Surfing Canopy Tours. The ziplines range from 37-95 metres and offer spectacular views from heights of 7 to 16 metres up in the forest canopy. Work together as a team and conquer your fear of heights with this awesome off the beaten track attraction.

### 3. Go Snorkelling Or Diving At Low Isles

The sandy coral cay islands of Low Isles are located just 15 kilometres off shore from Port Douglas and are a great place to spend the day exploring this small section of the Great Barrier Reef. With two small islands to swim and snorkel between Low Isles and Woody Island – you will have plenty to explore (guided tours are the best way to experience the Low Isles). You will find beautiful coral reefs with a large variety of fish, molluscs, sea cucumbers and other sea creatures along with the odd turtle if you are lucky!

### 4. Spot Crocs In The Wild With Hartley’s Crocodile Adventures

Now this may not sound too child-friendly, but the folks at Hartley’s Crocodile Adventures guarantee that their tours of Hartley’s Lagoon on their specially designed boats really is okay for people of all ages. There’s nothing quite like the sound of this ancient beast’s jaws snapping around its next meal – an experience you’ll never forget it and your kids will love it! The Hartley’s Crocodile Adventures complex is located just 25 minutes’ drive from Port Douglas and there are a number of other wildlife on site you are able to view. 

### 5. Visit Daintree Rainforest

Located on the northeast coast of Queensland, 1.5 hours drive from Port Douglas is the fantastic Daintree Rainforest. The oldest surviving tropical rainforest in the world, Daintree measures around 1,200 square kilometres (460 square miles) it is part of the largest continuous area of tropical rainforest on the Australian continent. Stop by the Daintree Discovery Centre for more information on visiting Daintree or see below for a more in-depth look at things to do at the Daintree Rainforest.

### 6. Check Out The Beach In Port Douglas

Building sand castles or playing beach cricket on the perfectly soft white sands of Four Mile Beach will easily keep your family entertained for the majority of the day. When you have had enough sun and sand for the day wander through the rainforest on a 1 kilometre walking track linking Four Mile Beach and Rex Smeal Park and head on over to Macrossan Street and explore the cafes and boutique shops.

### 7. Go Go Great Barrier Reef

The famous Great Barrier Reef stretches along the coast of Queensland in north eastern Australia for a good 2,300 kilometres (1,500 miles). It is the largest living thing on Planet Earth – you can actually see it from outer space! This famous Reef is home to over 600 species of hard and soft coral and and let’s not forget the Reef’s countless breed of fish, turtles, dolphins and sharks. No wonder it is one of the most attractions in Australia. There are numerous guided tours and experiences to choose from to make the most of the Great Barrier Reef.

## Port Douglas To Daintree Rainforest – An In-Depth Look 

The Daintree Rainforest is more than a just a quick trip which is why we have dedicated this extra block of text to discussing this great attraction in more detail – Daintree Rainforest really is a whole world of wonder ready for you to explore. 

### Daintree Discovery Centre 

The Daintree Discovery Centre is the first stop for any travellers and will keep you and your kids busy for some time (check out their headsets explaining all the cool facts about the rainforest in a language they will understand).

### Guided Tours Of Daintree Rainforest

Joining a guided tour to Daintree Rainforest can be the best way to take on the challenge of fitting in as much as you can in a day at a this amazing natural attraction. It can be a bit of a pain having to get up and ready for an early morning start (most tours will head off fairly early to get a good on the day as they cover so much in one day, that you really do need to start early).

Daintree Rainforest Tours from Port Douglas provide excellent local guides, transportation to and around parts of the rainforest and some added extras such as river cruises and guided walks as well as the ferry crossing that takes you across the Daintree River into the Daintree National Park. Some points of interest to look for in Daintree Rainforest Tours are:

### Cape Tribulation

Departing the ferry, Cape Tribulation is a common starting point where there is a beautiful little beach. Further up the beach, there is a great lookout from which you are able to take some great photos. 

### River Cruises
